caption: About-face is now a TRUE conversio (#541): every soldier turns in place and holds its OWN world position for the whole turn -- only facing flips. A prior fix (#519) stopped the block from pivoting 180 deg about its centre, but a state dump proved it still relabelled bodies on completion, so soldiers silently traded places with their formation-mirror counterpart (front rank <-> rear rank) even though the aggregate footprint looked unchanged. That relabel is gone: the turn now folds its rotation into the same formation-angle offset the quarter-turn uses, so soldier_world_slots reproduces each body's own pre-turn slot under the new facing. Verified with a per-soldier-index state dump (not just aggregate bbox/centroid) at tick 0 and after the turn completes: 40/40 soldiers now hold their own position, 0/40 swap.
